comment.body: Bottom line: The net short in CFTC data is real but doesn't capture CTA-specific bullish mechanics. This bifurcation explains resilient rallies—CTAs provide "dumb money" flow support while others hedge. If S&P holds above 6100-6200, expect more CTA buying; below that, they could amplify selling.

comment.body:
Hi Daphne, 

In the July 22nd article, Alma had written these lines and also included a chart. The chart shows a projected fall towards mid and end of August. 

"This still holds for me in the very near term (tech can go even lower than 10%), and my risk target remains the August - Sept window, post-FOMC event as catalsyst"


My read is that post july FOMC ( July 31st) to August 23 ( Jackson hole). From a macro level you are right that rate cut expectations are very low for July. Two fed governors ( Trump picks) are dissenting with Powell and by Jackson hole this might grow further. Jackson hole will not be an official rate cut meeting however market might keenly observe. Currently rate cut odds for September are at 60% and by Jackson hole the chorus might grow further. So by Sept 16th FOMC if a rate cut comes, the market can rally back from the projected fall in August. Seasonality wise also post 
election years August is one of the weakest months. 

Timing the exact drop and path is pretty hard and does it fall 10% in a week vs slow grind over a month until Sept FOMC. For that we need the maestro to be back and maybe we get additional guidance post August 1st.
